etymology.description: 爪(爪) (zhǎo, "hand") + 又(又) (yòu, "hand") + 石(石) (shí, "stone") = borrowing concept indicated by two hands holding a stone -> phonetic value for 'jiǎ'
etymology.interpretation: The character 叚 combines the imagery of two hands, 爪(爪) and 又(又), with the phonetic element 石(石), representing the idea of borrowing through handling a stone. This reflects its conceptual origin which relates to lending or borrowing.
